raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year> XXXX charges were made to a card issued in the name of my spouse XXXX XXXX, please see credit card statement attached. In addition to these XXXX charges a fourth charge was made to the card from the same merchant but was declined by the Citi card administrator. Upon review of these XXXX  charges I initiated a fraud alert with Citicard and disputed the charges which we never made. We've never purchased any goods from either of these retailers. 

Please note while XXXX XXXX has authorization to use the card ending in XXXX, she never uses it since it is only for emergencies. These fraudulent charges were the only time this card was used! This fact was also reported to Citi. 

As you can see from the notice Citi sent for more information that was answered, and from the final disposition they are denying fraud since they stated ; " The cards XXXX chip was used to make the disputed transactions at a chip enabled card reader. '' And that we verified the card was in our possession when the transaction occurred. 

I'm certain that you will agree there's virtually no way a card could be presented in XXXX PA 2 times then presented same day at XXXX Illinois? 

XXXX closed the fraud inquiry and is now looking for us to pay for the {$1100.00}, which is clearly fraudulent. 

If not fraudulent why did Citi decline a XXXX transaction on the same day from the same vendor and how can the card be presented at XXXX PA and XXXX Illinois on the same day. 

Please assis us in resolving this matter with Citi. They have declined any responsibility!
